issues.claim: Analog not reconstructible from chapter alone
issues.objection: Removed-verse test for 7.21: if 7.21 were removed from the source pack, the chapter alone would not produce this specific engineering analog. The analog depends on the verse's specific structural claim (thread 3 (māyā-and-four-devotees / faith-made-firm doctrine)), which is not stated equivalently elsewhere in the chapter. Tags (operator-system-coupling, deliberate-action, iteration-loop) anchor on this verse's specific claim. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims a personal divine agency (vidadhāmy aham — I make firm); engineering analog stays at structural-pattern scope. Counter-example explicitly carves out structurally-inappropriate technique-fit so the analog does not collapse into 'commit to anything.'

notes: Removed-verse test for 7.21: analog is anchored on this verse's specific structural claim, not on chapter-level material alone. PASS.
